mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively over a 20-day horizon, reflecting investor optimism about future profits.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are negative or flat (within 0.1%) in more than 55% of periods where RSAFS shows sustained month-over-month growth above 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the unemployment rate below a certain threshold signals a tightening labor market, which typically precedes wage inflation and strong consumer spending, boosting S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon due to improved economic outlook.
evidence_refs: fact:low unemployment typically correlates with economic expansion
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 4.5% during an identified late-cycle expansion.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is low and housing starts are increasing, it indicates an accommodative monetary environment supporting real estate activity, which has spillover effects into broader economic growth and equity markets, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of periods where both FEDFUNDS is below 1.0% and HOUST shows strong 3-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust industrial production growth, corporate earnings tend to be strong due to high capacity utilization and demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon. This effect is particularly pronounced in expansionary regimes.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances during confirmed economic expansion periods, or if Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in inflation (CPIAUCSL) above a certain threshold signals overheating in the economy, which typically prompts central bank tightening and higher discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:historical inverse correlation between high inflation and equity returns
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% on an annualized 3-month basis.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates a healthy labor market and robust economic activity, which supports corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month growth and S&P 500 20-day returns is consistently negative or zero (Rank IC < 0.01) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth is strong and industrial production is also expanding, it signifies a robust and balanced economic expansion where both demand and supply are healthy, leading to strong corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 30% of periods where both GDP and Industrial Production show strong year-over-year growth.

mechanism_narrative: A rising Federal Funds Rate during a period of low inflation indicates that the central bank is proactively tightening monetary policy, which can dampen economic activity and increase the cost of capital, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:central bank tightening often precedes market corrections
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with contained inflation
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are consistently positive (average return > 0.1%) during periods of FEDFUNDS hikes when CPIAUCSL 12-month growth is below 2.0%.
